---
title: "Golf Balance Device Research"
---

## Overview

This site documents the research background and experimental design for a preliminary 
trial of a proprioceptive balance training device applied to golf swing weight transfer.

The device was originally designed for ski training. This project explores whether the 
same proprioceptive mechanism — constraining foot pressure to the medial edge — 
produces measurable changes in golf swing weight transfer patterns.

## Research Questions

1. Does training with the device produce measurable changes in centre of pressure 
   timing and magnitude during the golf swing?
2. Are any changes in weight transfer pattern associated with changes in ball outcome 
   (direction, consistency)?
3. How does the magnitude of change compare to natural swing-to-swing variability?

## Project Structure

- **Literature Register** — systematic review of relevant research across four areas
- **Experiment Design** — protocol for preliminary trial
- **Outcome Measures** — rationale for chosen measures
- **Analysis Plan** — pre-specified statistical approach

## Status

- [ ] Literature review — in progress
- [ ] Experiment design — draft
- [ ] Outcome measures — draft  
- [ ] Analysis plan — not started
- [ ] Ethics consideration — not started
